name: Christian Community
description: Learn how to encourage, counsel, and live in unity with fellow believers
lessons: [{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"1 Thessalonians 5:14","text":"And we urge you, brothers, admonish the idle, encourage the fainthearted, help the weak, be patient with them all.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-cc-1-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Every Christian should be engaged in encouragement counseling. The best encouragement counseling uses different tones of conversation. This ministry happens in the foyer, standing beside your car outside, and during Sunday afternoon lunches. It is characterized by spiritual admonishment, encouragement, support, and patience.","title":"Encouragement Counseling"},"id":"dev-cc-1-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Galatians 6:1-2","text":"Brothers, if anyone is caught in any transgression, you who are spiritual should restore him in a spirit of gentleness. Keep watch on yourself, lest you too be tempted. Bear one another's burdens, and so fulfill the law of Christ.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-cc-1-3","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Paul encouraged the Thessalonian believers to encourage each other in certain ways for certain types of difficulties. For the lazy and unruly, the believers were to admonish, warn, or advise. For the discouraged, the believers were to encourage and comfort them. For the weak or sick folk, the believers were to cleave to and support them. All of these counseling sessions have different conversation tones.","title":"Different People, Different Approaches"},"id":"dev-cc-1-4","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Paul instructs believers to admonish (warn or advise) those who are idle or unruly. This requires a different tone than comforting the discouraged.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Comfort and encourage them"},{"id":"b","text":"Admonish and warn them"},{"id":"c","text":"Leave them alone"},{"id":"d","text":"Report them to leadership"}],"question":"According to 1 Thessalonians 5:14, what approach should we use with those who are idle or unruly?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-cc-1-5","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Hebrews 3:13","text":"But exhort one another every day, as long as it is called today, that none of you may be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-cc-1-6","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"In the 21st century local church, believers interact with hurting, disobedient, and discouraged believers on a weekly or daily basis. The counseling approach and attitude must be appropriate for each individual. Not one approach works in every counseling situation. As we interact with believers on a day-to-day basis, let us be encouragers, admonishers, comforters, supporters, and advisors.","title":"Daily Application"},"id":"dev-cc-1-7","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"For the discouraged, Paul says to encourage and comfort them. They need support, not correction.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Admonish and warn them"},{"id":"b","text":"Encourage and comfort them"},{"id":"c","text":"Ignore their feelings"},{"id":"d","text":"Give them space"}],"question":"What approach should we use with someone who is fainthearted (discouraged)?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-cc-1-8","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Colossians 3:16","text":"Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly, teaching and admonishing one another in all wisdom, singing psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, with thankfulness in your hearts to God.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-cc-1-9","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"c","explanation":"We should restore others in a spirit of gentleness, keeping watch on ourselves lest we also be tempted.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A spirit of judgment"},{"id":"b","text":"A spirit of superiority"},{"id":"c","text":"A spirit of gentleness"},{"id":"d","text":"A spirit of anger"}],"question":"According to Galatians 6:1, in what spirit should we restore someone caught in transgression?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-cc-1-10","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"Think of someone in your life who needs encouragement. What approach does their situation call for—admonishment, comfort, or faithful support? How can you minister to them this week?"},"id":"dev-cc-1-11","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Learn how to encourage different people in different ways","id":"dev-cc-1","isCompleted":false,"order":1,"title":"Counseling in the Church Corridor","trackId":"devotional-christian-community","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Acts 2:46-47","text":"And day by day, attending the temple together and breaking bread in their homes, they received their food with glad and generous hearts, praising God and having favor with all the people.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-cc-2-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"The Greek word ekklesia is a descriptive word meaning assembly or an assembly of the citizens regularly summoned. The Christian use describes the called-out assembly that gathers on Sunday morning—a gathered group of believers meeting together for the glory of God and the proclamation of the Gospel.","title":"The Meaning of Ekklesia"},"id":"dev-cc-2-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Hebrews 10:24-25","text":"And let us consider how to stir up one another to love and good works, not neglecting to meet together, as is the habit of some, but encouraging one another, and all the more as you see the Day drawing near.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-cc-2-3","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Ekklesia means an assembly or gathering of citizens regularly summoned. It emphasizes the people gathering, not a building.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A building for worship"},{"id":"b","text":"An assembly of citizens regularly summoned"},{"id":"c","text":"A religious organization"},{"id":"d","text":"A social club"}],"question":"What does the Greek word ekklesia primarily mean?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-cc-2-4","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Acts 7:38","text":"This is the one who was in the congregation in the wilderness with the angel who spoke to him at Mount Sinai, and with our fathers. He received living oracles to give to us.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-cc-2-5","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Throughout Acts, the focus of the church is praising God and the glory of Christ. The early believers gathered daily, attending the temple together and breaking bread in their homes with glad and generous hearts. Their fellowship was marked by joy, generosity, and worship.","title":"The Focus of the Church"},"id":"dev-cc-2-6","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"We meet together to stir up one another to love and good works, encouraging one another as we see the Day approaching.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"To fulfill a religious duty"},{"id":"b","text":"To stir up one another to love and good works"},{"id":"c","text":"To maintain church membership"},{"id":"d","text":"To be seen by others"}],"question":"According to Hebrews 10:24-25, why should we not neglect meeting together?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-cc-2-7","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"2 Corinthians 8:23","text":"As for Titus, he is my partner and fellow worker for your benefit. And as for our brothers, they are messengers of the churches, the glory of Christ.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-cc-2-8","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"As a Christian, throughout the seasons of life, gathering with believers is critical for spiritual health. Purposing to live our lives in unity with our brothers in Christ should be the priority. The writer of Hebrews warns against the habit of neglecting to meet together.","title":"The Priority of Gathering"},"id":"dev-cc-2-9","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Psalm 133:1","text":"Behold, how good and pleasant it is when brothers dwell in unity!","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-cc-2-10","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"The early church received their food with glad and generous hearts, praising God and having favor with all the people.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Formal rituals and ceremonies"},{"id":"b","text":"Glad and generous hearts, praising God"},{"id":"c","text":"Strict rules and regulations"},{"id":"d","text":"Private, secretive meetings"}],"question":"What characterized the early church's gatherings in Acts 2?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-cc-2-11","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"How can you prioritize gathering with believers in your current season of life? What might need to change to make fellowship a higher priority?"},"id":"dev-cc-2-12","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Understand the purpose and priority of gathering with believers","id":"dev-cc-2","isCompleted":false,"order":2,"title":"Assembly of Citizens","trackId":"devotional-christian-community","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"text":"In communities around the world, people are gathering together. Some gather around a sport such as basketball or football. Others gather around a sale such as an auction or community yard sale. But Christians gather for a different purpose—a unifying goal and mission: to glorify God, evangelize the lost, and edify the saints.","title":"Why Do We Gather?"},"id":"dev-cc-3-1","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Hebrews 10:25","text":"Not neglecting to meet together, as is the habit of some, but encouraging one another, and all the more as you see the Day drawing near.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-cc-3-2","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Psalm 117:1","text":"Praise the LORD, all nations! Extol him, all peoples!","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-cc-3-3","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"The mission of the church is to glorify God through worship of Him. As Christians gather around the world, there must be a purpose for meeting. If we were coming together as just friends, why would we not watch football? There must be a unifying purpose. Praise is the desire of God from His creatures. All nations should praise the Lord.","title":"Mission #1: Glorify God Through Worship"},"id":"dev-cc-3-4","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Revelation 5:12","text":"Saying with a loud voice, Worthy is the Lamb who was slain, to receive power and wealth and wisdom and might and honor and glory and blessing!","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-cc-3-5","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"The primary mission of the church is to glorify God through worship. He is worthy to receive power, wealth, wisdom, might, honor, glory, and blessing.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Building programs"},{"id":"b","text":"Glorifying God through worship"},{"id":"c","text":"Community service"},{"id":"d","text":"Political activism"}],"question":"What is the first aspect of the church's mission?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-cc-3-6","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Acts 13:2-3","text":"While they were worshiping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them. Then after fasting and praying they laid their hands on them and sent them off.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-cc-3-7","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"The church's outreach method outlined in Acts is through missionaries. At the beginning of Paul's first missionary journey, he was chosen by the Lord and sent out by the church at Antioch. God is the one choosing and calling those saints to glorify Himself on the foreign field, but the church is the tool God uses to send workers to spread the Gospel.","title":"Mission #2: Evangelize the Lost Through Outreach"},"id":"dev-cc-3-8","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Matthew 28:19-20","text":"Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, teaching them to observe all that I have commanded you.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-cc-3-9","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"While worshiping and fasting, the Holy Spirit directed the church to set apart Barnabas and Saul. After fasting and praying, they sent them off.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Through a vote and election"},{"id":"b","text":"Through worship, fasting, and prayer"},{"id":"c","text":"Through financial incentives"},{"id":"d","text":"Through random selection"}],"question":"According to Acts 13, how did the church at Antioch send out missionaries?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-cc-3-10","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"2 Timothy 2:2","text":"And what you have heard from me in the presence of many witnesses entrust to faithful men, who will be able to teach others also.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-cc-3-11","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"In Matthew's account of the Great Commission, Jesus says outreach to the world should include discipleship. This command extends past the apostles to every believer, as Jesus promises I am with you always, to the end of the age. Following Jesus' example, the church should clearly proclaim the Word of God doctrinally, applicably, and clearly.","title":"Mission #3: Edify the Saints Through Discipleship"},"id":"dev-cc-3-12","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"The church's mission is to glorify God through worship, evangelize the lost through outreach, and edify the saints through discipleship.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Build buildings, raise money, grow membership"},{"id":"b","text":"Glorify God, evangelize the lost, edify the saints"},{"id":"c","text":"Preach, pray, sing"},{"id":"d","text":"Study, fellowship, serve"}],"question":"What are the three aspects of the church's mission?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-cc-3-13","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Ephesians 4:11-12","text":"And he gave the apostles, the prophets, the evangelists, the shepherds and teachers, to equip the saints for the work of ministry, for building up the body of Christ.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-cc-3-14","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"prompt":"Which aspect of the church's mission—glorifying God, evangelizing, or edifying—do you need to be more involved in? What is one step you can take this week?"},"id":"dev-cc-3-15","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Discover the threefold mission God has given His church","id":"dev-cc-3","isCompleted":false,"order":3,"title":"The Mission of the Church","trackId":"devotional-christian-community","xpReward":60}]

name: Foundations of Faith
description: Explore core truths about God's nature and our hope in Him
lessons: [{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Titus 1:2","text":"In hope of eternal life, which God, who never lies, promised before the ages began.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-1-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"The Christian's hope of redemption and eternal life relies on the honesty of God. Paul's greeting in his letter to Titus contains the high-impact phrase God, who never lies. This phrase affirms that God not only does not lie, but He cannot lie because of His holy nature.","title":"The Christian's Secure Hope"},"id":"dev-ff-1-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Numbers 23:19","text":"God is not man, that he should lie, or a son of man, that he should change his mind. Has he said, and will he not do it? Or has he spoken, and will he not fulfill it?","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-1-3","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"God is not man, that he should lie. His very nature as God makes lying impossible for Him.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Because He chooses not to"},{"id":"b","text":"Because He is not man"},{"id":"c","text":"Because lying is forbidden"},{"id":"d","text":"Because angels watch Him"}],"question":"According to Numbers 23:19, why does God not lie?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-ff-1-4","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Hebrews 6:17-18","text":"So when God desired to show more convincingly to the heirs of the promise the unchangeable character of his purpose, he guaranteed it with an oath, so that by two unchangeable things, in which it is impossible for God to lie, we who have fled for refuge might have strong encouragement.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-1-5","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"There is discussion over God's nature concerning lying. The KJV uses the phrase cannot lie but the ESV uses never lies. If God is pure, holy, and will always stay the same, He cannot lie because of His preexisting, unchanging holy nature. Hebrews 6:18 affirms it is impossible for God to lie.","title":"Cannot Lie vs. Never Lies"},"id":"dev-ff-1-6","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"God's inability to lie flows from His unchanging holy nature. He is pure and holy and will always stay the same.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"It means God is limited"},{"id":"b","text":"It shows lying is impossible given His unchanging holy nature"},{"id":"c","text":"It means God has rules to follow"},{"id":"d","text":"It is not significant"}],"question":"Why is it significant that God cannot lie?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-ff-1-7","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"2 Timothy 2:13","text":"If we are faithless, he remains faithful—for he cannot deny himself.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-1-8","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"God has promised that because of His inability to lie, we can have secure confidence in eternal life. This concept is critical to the eternal security of the believer. When unbelievers place their faith in Jesus Christ, they can be assured that they will live with Christ in heaven forever because God cannot deny Himself or break His promises.","title":"Eternal Security in God's Honesty"},"id":"dev-ff-1-9","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Romans 3:4","text":"Let God be true though every one were a liar, as it is written, That you may be justified in your words, and prevail when you are judged.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-1-10","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"a","explanation":"God guaranteed His promise with an oath, so that by these two unchangeable things, we might have strong encouragement.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"God's promise and His oath"},{"id":"b","text":"Heaven and earth"},{"id":"c","text":"Faith and works"},{"id":"d","text":"The law and prophets"}],"question":"According to Hebrews 6:17-18, what two unchangeable things give us strong encouragement?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-ff-1-11","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"How does knowing that God cannot lie affect your confidence in the promises of Scripture? What promise do you need to trust more fully today?"},"id":"dev-ff-1-12","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Discover why God's inability to lie secures your salvation","id":"dev-ff-1","isCompleted":false,"order":1,"title":"The Honesty of God","trackId":"devotional-foundations","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Galatians 4:6","text":"And because you are sons, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying, Abba! Father!","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-2-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"God promised to Abraham that the nations would be blessed through his seed. Galatians outlines that those who are in Christ are Abraham's seed and heirs according to the promise (Gal. 3:29). This places believers into the family of God with all the privileges of sons.","title":"Heirs According to the Promise"},"id":"dev-ff-2-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Galatians 3:29","text":"And if you are Christ's, then you are Abraham's offspring, heirs according to promise.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-2-3","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"If you are Christ's, then you are Abraham's offspring, heirs according to promise—regardless of ethnicity.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Only ethnic Jews"},{"id":"b","text":"Those who are Christ's"},{"id":"c","text":"Only the apostles"},{"id":"d","text":"Those who follow the law"}],"question":"According to Galatians 3:29, who are Abraham's offspring?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-ff-2-4","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Romans 8:15-16","text":"For you did not receive the spirit of slavery to fall back into fear, but you have received the Spirit of adoption as sons, by whom we cry, Abba! Father! The Spirit himself bears witness with our spirit that we are children of God.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-2-5","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"God sent His Son into the world to redeem those in bondage under the law. He then placed those who believe into a right relationship with God. The Holy Spirit was promised by Jesus (John 14:26) and fulfilled at Pentecost. He is placed into our hearts, giving us intimate access to the Father.","title":"The Spirit in Our Hearts"},"id":"dev-ff-2-6","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"John 14:26","text":"But the Helper, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, he will teach you all things and bring to your remembrance all that I have said to you.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-2-7","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"The Spirit himself bears witness with our spirit that we are children of God, giving us assurance of our relationship with the Father.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Condemns us for our sins"},{"id":"b","text":"Bears witness that we are children of God"},{"id":"c","text":"Forces us to obey"},{"id":"d","text":"Keeps us in fear"}],"question":"What does the Holy Spirit do according to Romans 8:16?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-ff-2-8","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Galatians 4:4-5","text":"But when the fullness of time had come, God sent forth his Son, born of woman, born under the law, to redeem those who were under the law, so that we might receive adoption as sons.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-2-9","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"The term Abba is Aramaic for father and gives the idea of papa—a deeply personal, filial term. This ministry of the Holy Spirit gives the child of God personal access to God the Father. Christians today must be comforted with the security of their fatherly relationship with their Heavenly Father.","title":"Abba, Father"},"id":"dev-ff-2-10","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Abba is Aramaic for father, conveying the intimate idea of papa—a personal, familial term showing our close relationship with God.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A formal title for God"},{"id":"b","text":"An Aramaic term meaning papa or daddy"},{"id":"c","text":"A Hebrew word for king"},{"id":"d","text":"A Greek word for master"}],"question":"What does the term Abba mean?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-ff-2-11","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Romans 8:14","text":"For all who are led by the Spirit of God are sons of God.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-2-12","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"prompt":"How does it change your prayer life to think of God as your Abba (Papa, Father)? How can you share this hope with someone this week?"},"id":"dev-ff-2-13","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Understand your relationship with God as His child","id":"dev-ff-2","isCompleted":false,"order":2,"title":"The Comforting Work of the Holy Spirit","trackId":"devotional-foundations","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Matthew 5:9","text":"Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called sons of God.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-3-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"The Sermon on the Mount is a great example of a futuristic passage that is applicable to everyday Christian living and preparation for life in the Millennial Kingdom. Jesus was presenting the Kingdom of God, offering principles of godly living that apply to our daily walk.","title":"A Futuristic Passage with Present Application"},"id":"dev-ff-3-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Matthew 5:3-6","text":"Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. Blessed are those who mourn, for they shall be comforted. Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth. Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they shall be satisfied.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-3-3","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Blessed describes those who are happy or fortunate because they are recipients of God's favor—a state of spiritual well-being.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Materially wealthy"},{"id":"b","text":"Happy or fortunate in God's favor"},{"id":"c","text":"Free from troubles"},{"id":"d","text":"Popular with others"}],"question":"What does blessed mean in the Beatitudes?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-ff-3-4","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Matthew 5:7-8","text":"Blessed are the merciful, for they shall receive mercy. Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-3-5","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Because modern Christians are not living in the Millennial Kingdom, some might think these standards will not work in a sin-cursed world. However, Jesus was offering principles of godly living for daily application. Matthew 5:9, for example, is not about earning heaven—it emphasizes the spirit of peacemaking.","title":"Principles for Today"},"id":"dev-ff-3-6","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"c","explanation":"The Beatitudes offer principles of godly living that believers can apply to daily life now as we prepare for Christ's return.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Following these commands earns salvation"},{"id":"b","text":"They are only for Israel in the future"},{"id":"c","text":"They are principles of godly living we can apply now"},{"id":"d","text":"They are outdated and no longer relevant"}],"question":"What is the primary application of the Beatitudes for Christians today?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-ff-3-7","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Matthew 5:10-12","text":"Blessed are those who are persecuted for righteousness' sake, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. Blessed are you when others revile you and persecute you... Rejoice and be glad, for your reward is great in heaven.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-3-8","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"2 Corinthians 13:11","text":"Finally, brothers, rejoice. Aim for restoration, comfort one another, agree with one another, live in peace; and the God of love and peace will be with you.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-3-9","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"As we continue striving to follow the Spirit our Lord left behind, we must prepare our lifestyle for harmonious relationships with our fellow man. Paul echoes this in 2 Corinthians 13:11: Be of good comfort, be of one mind, live in peace; and the God of love and peace will be with you.","title":"Preparing for Harmonious Relationships"},"id":"dev-ff-3-10","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"a","explanation":"Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called sons of God. Peacemaking reflects our Father's character.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Sons of God"},{"id":"b","text":"Servants of the church"},{"id":"c","text":"Blessed among men"},{"id":"d","text":"Leaders of nations"}],"question":"According to Matthew 5:9, what will peacemakers be called?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-ff-3-11","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Romans 12:18","text":"If possible, so far as it depends on you, live peaceably with all.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-ff-3-12","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"prompt":"In what relationship or situation can you actively be a peacemaker this week? How can you apply the spirit of the Beatitudes to your daily life?"},"id":"dev-ff-3-13","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Apply kingdom principles to everyday Christian living","id":"dev-ff-3","isCompleted":false,"order":3,"title":"Sermon on the Mount Life Preparation","trackId":"devotional-foundations","xpReward":50}]

name: Faithful Living
description: Practical wisdom for living out your faith with integrity
lessons: [{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Romans 16:19","text":"For your obedience is known to all, so that I rejoice over you, but I want you to be wise as to what is good and innocent as to what is evil.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-1-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"The Roman believers had a particular testimony—they were known for obedience to Scripture. Despite having battled dissension and false doctrine, the believers remained strong in their personal lives. They consistently obeyed the Apostle's doctrine, causing Paul to rejoice.","title":"A Testimony of Obedience"},"id":"dev-fl-1-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Philippians 2:15","text":"That you may be blameless and innocent, children of God without blemish in the midst of a crooked and twisted generation, among whom you shine as lights in the world.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-1-3","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Paul calls believers to be wise as to what is good and innocent as to what is evil—knowing the good deeply while remaining untainted by evil.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Wealthy and powerful"},{"id":"b","text":"Wise to good and innocent to evil"},{"id":"c","text":"Popular and influential"},{"id":"d","text":"Quiet and hidden"}],"question":"According to Romans 16:19, what two qualities should characterize Christians?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-fl-1-4","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Matthew 10:16","text":"Behold, I am sending you out as sheep in the midst of wolves, so be wise as serpents and innocent as doves.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-1-5","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Paul acknowledged their hard work in living godly lives in an impure world, but he also challenged them: to live godly, obedient lives, they must be wise to good and innocent to evil. Instead of dwelling on evil, they were to focus on what is good and truthful.","title":"Wise to Good, Innocent to Evil"},"id":"dev-fl-1-6","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Philippians 4:8","text":"Finally, brothers, whatever is true, whatever is honorable, whatever is just, whatever is pure, whatever is lovely, whatever is commendable, if there is any excellence, if there is anything worthy of praise, think about these things.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-1-7","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Paul instructs us to think about things that are true, honorable, just, pure, lovely, commendable, excellent, and praiseworthy.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Whatever is popular and trending"},{"id":"b","text":"Whatever is true, honorable, just, pure, and lovely"},{"id":"c","text":"Whatever makes them successful"},{"id":"d","text":"Whatever their friends think about"}],"question":"According to Philippians 4:8, what should believers think about?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-fl-1-8","type":"question"},{"data":{"text":"Believers who want to maintain the unity of the church and reject false teaching must be discerning of the truth and error they ingest. Instead of filling our minds with TikTok theology, we must immerse ourselves in the truth of God's Word. A clear testimony of obedience to God's Word is essential.","title":"Discerning Truth from Error"},"id":"dev-fl-1-9","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"1 Peter 1:14-15","text":"As obedient children, do not be conformed to the passions of your former ignorance, but as he who called you is holy, you also be holy in all your conduct.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-1-10","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Jesus calls us to be shrewd and discerning (wise as serpents) while maintaining purity and harmlessness (innocent as doves).","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Be cunning and deceitful"},{"id":"b","text":"Be shrewd and discerning while remaining pure"},{"id":"c","text":"Avoid all contact with the world"},{"id":"d","text":"Trust everyone you meet"}],"question":"What does Jesus mean by being wise as serpents and innocent as doves?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-fl-1-11","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"What are you filling your mind with? How can you become more wise to good and innocent to evil in your media consumption and relationships?"},"id":"dev-fl-1-12","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Learn how obedience and wisdom define a faithful life","id":"dev-fl-1","isCompleted":false,"order":1,"title":"The Testimony of a Christian","trackId":"devotional-faithful-living","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 4:19","text":"The way of the wicked is like deep darkness; they do not know over what they stumble.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-2-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"The way of the wicked is sheer darkness. They are in denial of God, walking towards a certain end, carrying those around them with them. It is like walking through a cave without a lamp—utter, gloomy darkness. They are blind toward the fear of the Lord and the knowledge of Him.","title":"Walking in Darkness"},"id":"dev-fl-2-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 4:18","text":"But the path of the righteous is like the light of dawn, which shines brighter and brighter until full day.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-2-3","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"The righteous walk in light that grows brighter and brighter, while the wicked stumble in deep darkness.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Narrow vs. wide"},{"id":"b","text":"Light of dawn vs. deep darkness"},{"id":"c","text":"Easy vs. difficult"},{"id":"d","text":"Short vs. long"}],"question":"How does Proverbs contrast the path of the righteous with the way of the wicked?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-fl-2-4","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 15:9","text":"The way of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD, but he loves him who pursues righteousness.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-2-5","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Not only is the way of the wicked joyless, but it is an abomination to the Lord. Not just their actions, but even the thoughts of the wicked are an abomination (Prov. 15:26). This fact alone should make any decent person utterly avoid the path of the wicked. The opposite—the way of righteousness—is a path loved by the Lord.","title":"An Abomination to the Lord"},"id":"dev-fl-2-6","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 15:26","text":"The thoughts of the wicked are an abomination to the LORD, but gracious words are pure.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-2-7","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"The LORD loves him who pursues righteousness—those who actively seek to live according to His ways.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Those who are wealthy"},{"id":"b","text":"Those who pursue righteousness"},{"id":"c","text":"Those who are religious"},{"id":"d","text":"Those who are powerful"}],"question":"According to Proverbs 15:9, what does the LORD love?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-fl-2-8","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 12:5","text":"The thoughts of the righteous are just; the counsels of the wicked are deceitful.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-2-9","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"There are two main characteristics of the way of the wicked: it is deceitful and violent. They deceive themselves and others. Their counsel is deceitful, likely for their own gain. They sow deceit and are seduced by their own seductiveness. Their path leads to violence for themselves and those around them.","title":"Deceit and Violence"},"id":"dev-fl-2-10","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 12:26","text":"One who is righteous is a guide to his neighbor, but the way of the wicked leads them astray.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-2-11","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Proverbs portrays the way of the wicked as characterized by deceit (toward self and others) and violence (harming self and others).","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Wealth and power"},{"id":"b","text":"Deceit and violence"},{"id":"c","text":"Pleasure and success"},{"id":"d","text":"Religion and tradition"}],"question":"What are the two main characteristics of the way of the wicked?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-fl-2-12","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"Are there any areas of your life where you're walking in darkness rather than in the light? What steps can you take to pursue righteousness instead?"},"id":"dev-fl-2-13","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Understand why Scripture describes wickedness as utter darkness","id":"dev-fl-2","isCompleted":false,"order":2,"title":"The Way of the Wicked","trackId":"devotional-faithful-living","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 10:7","text":"The memory of the righteous is a blessing, but the name of the wicked will rot.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-3-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"In Proverbs, the concept of versus is very prominent through antithetical parallelism—contrasting the hope of the righteous with the wicked. For the righteous there is joy, but for the wicked only the hope of perishing. Proverbs displays cursing for the wicked and blessing for the righteous at four levels: logical, providential, spiritual, and eternal.","title":"Cursing vs. Blessing"},"id":"dev-fl-3-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 3:33","text":"The LORD's curse is on the house of the wicked, but he blesses the dwelling of the righteous.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-3-3","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"The memory of the righteous is a blessing—their legacy endures and blesses others even after death.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"It is forgotten"},{"id":"b","text":"It is a blessing"},{"id":"c","text":"It is disputed"},{"id":"d","text":"It fades away"}],"question":"According to Proverbs 10:7, what happens to the memory of the righteous?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-fl-3-4","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 10:3","text":"The LORD does not let the righteous go hungry, but he thwarts the craving of the wicked.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-3-5","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"After the wicked are dead, their name will be like putrid carrion, but the righteous will be blessing others even after their death. Their good works not only follow them, but live behind them. As one commentator put it, The good man helps to make others holy whilst he is lying in the grave.","title":"A Lasting Legacy"},"id":"dev-fl-3-6","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 11:18","text":"The wicked earns deceptive wages, but one who sows righteousness gets a sure reward.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-3-7","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"c","explanation":"The LORD blesses the dwelling of the righteous, while His curse is on the house of the wicked.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"It is cursed"},{"id":"b","text":"It is hidden"},{"id":"c","text":"It is blessed by the LORD"},{"id":"d","text":"It is forgotten"}],"question":"What does Proverbs 3:33 say about the dwelling of the righteous?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-fl-3-8","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 10:11","text":"The mouth of the righteous is a fountain of life, but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-3-9","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Another contrast between the righteous and the wicked is life vs. death. The wicked receive Sheol, utter destruction. Their reward is punishment and their fear will destroy them. In contrast, the righteous will have life rather than death, security rather than whirlwind. The righteous shall never be removed (Prov. 10:30).","title":"Life vs. Death"},"id":"dev-fl-3-10","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 11:23","text":"The desire of the righteous ends only in good; the expectation of the wicked in wrath.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-3-11","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"The desire of the righteous ends only in good, while the expectation of the wicked ends in wrath.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Frustration"},{"id":"b","text":"Only good"},{"id":"c","text":"Uncertainty"},{"id":"d","text":"Disappointment"}],"question":"According to Proverbs 11:23, what is the end of the righteous's desire?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-fl-3-12","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"What kind of legacy are you building? How can the wisdom of Proverbs shape your daily choices toward righteousness and lasting blessing?"},"id":"dev-fl-3-13","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Explore the stark contrast between blessing and cursing in Proverbs","id":"dev-fl-3","isCompleted":false,"order":3,"title":"The Righteous vs. The Wicked","trackId":"devotional-faithful-living","xpReward":55},{"content":[{"data":{"text":"Throughout Proverbs, the wise authors strive to convince their readers to choose the wise and righteous path. The basic premise is that the wicked will fall and be destroyed; however, the righteous will be lifted through wisdom from God. This does not mean the righteous never fall, but they will have a good end.","title":"The Purpose of Proverbs"},"id":"dev-fl-4-1","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 1:25-27","text":"Because you have ignored all my counsel and would have none of my reproof, I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when terror strikes you, when terror strikes you like a storm and your calamity comes like a whirlwind.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-4-2","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Those who ignore wisdom's counsel face terror that strikes like a storm and calamity that comes like a whirlwind.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"They eventually find peace"},{"id":"b","text":"Terror and calamity strike them"},{"id":"c","text":"They receive a second chance"},{"id":"d","text":"Nothing happens"}],"question":"What happens to those who ignore wisdom's counsel according to Proverbs 1?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-fl-4-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 3:25-26","text":"Do not be afraid of sudden terror or of the ruin of the wicked, when it comes, for the LORD will be your confidence and will keep your foot from being caught.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-4-4","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"The end of the wicked is desolation. The Hebrew word contains the idea of a tempest, storm, waste. Combined with sudden fear, it paints a picture of a sudden, destructive, frightening end. The wicked lust after their desires, but end in wrath. The way of the transgressor is hard (Prov. 13:15).","title":"Sudden Desolation"},"id":"dev-fl-4-5","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 2:22","text":"But the wicked will be cut off from the land, and the treacherous will be rooted out of it.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-4-6","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Good understanding giveth favour: but the way of transgressors is hard. Wickedness leads to a difficult, frustrating path.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Easy and pleasant"},{"id":"b","text":"Hard and difficult"},{"id":"c","text":"Short and painless"},{"id":"d","text":"Hidden and secret"}],"question":"According to Proverbs 13:15, what is the way of transgressors like?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-fl-4-7","type":"question"},{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 11:7","text":"When the wicked dies, his hope will perish, and the expectation of wealth perishes too.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-4-8","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"The wicked person's future is utterly horrible. When the wicked die, their hope perishes. Even if wicked men clasp one another's hands in strong confederacy, they will not go without God's punishment (Prov. 11:21). The wicked are submitted to a shortened life: The fear of the LORD prolongs days, but the years of the wicked shall be shortened (Prov. 10:27).","title":"No Escape from Judgment"},"id":"dev-fl-4-9","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 11:21","text":"Be assured, an evil person will not go unpunished, but the offspring of the righteous will be delivered.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-4-10","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Proverbs 10:27-28","text":"The fear of the LORD prolongs life, but the years of the wicked will be short. The hope of the righteous brings joy, but the expectation of the wicked will perish.","version":"ESV"},"id":"dev-fl-4-11","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"The fear of the LORD prolongs life, but the years of the wicked will be short. Reverence for God leads to blessing.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Causes anxiety"},{"id":"b","text":"Prolongs life"},{"id":"c","text":"Limits freedom"},{"id":"d","text":"Creates fear of death"}],"question":"What does the fear of the LORD do according to Proverbs 10:27?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"dev-fl-4-12","type":"question"},{"data":{"text":"To conclude, Proverbs shows the wicked in an enormously acrimonious life and death. The end of the wicked is horrendous and untimely—quite unlike the end of the righteous. The righteous are starkly contrasted with the wicked throughout. Therefore, throughout Proverbs, the wicked are shown in a negative manner to urge readers toward wisdom and righteousness.","title":"Conclusion"},"id":"dev-fl-4-13","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"prompt":"What is your hope built upon? How does the warning of Proverbs about the end of the wicked motivate you to pursue wisdom and righteousness?"},"id":"dev-fl-4-14","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Learn why the wise authors of Proverbs urge choosing righteousness","id":"dev-fl-4","isCompleted":false,"order":4,"title":"The End of the Wicked","trackId":"devotional-faithful-living","xpReward":60}]

name: Creation
description: See how God spoke the universe into existence in six literal days
lessons:
[{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Genesis 1:1","text":"In the beginning, God created the heavens and the earth.","version":"ESV"},"id":"creation-1-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Genesis 2:1-3","text":"Thus the heavens and the earth were finished, and all the host of them. And on the seventh day God finished his work that he had done, and he rested on the seventh day from all his work that he had done. So God blessed the seventh day and made it holy, because on it God rested from all his work that he had done in creation.","version":"ESV"},"id":"creation-1-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"The Bible begins with the foundational truth that God created everything. "The heavens and the earth" is a Hebrew expression meaning the entire universe—everything that exists. Before creation, there was only God. He did not create from pre-existing materials; He spoke everything into existence from nothing (ex nihilo). This sets the God of the Bible apart from all false gods.

We believe that the entire universe was brought into existence by the creating act of the Triune God. This is not a mythological account but historical truth that establishes God as sovereign over all that exists. The one who creates is greater than the creation—only God can bring something from nothing.","title":"The Creating God"},"id":"creation-1-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":""The heavens and the earth" means the entire universe. God created everything from nothing—there was no pre-existing matter.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"God reshaped existing materials"},{"id":"b","text":"God created the entire universe from nothing"},{"id":"c","text":"God only created the sky and ground"},{"id":"d","text":"Angels helped God create"}],"question":"What does "God created the heavens and the earth" mean?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"creation-1-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Before creation, only the eternal Triune God existed. He brought everything else into being by His word.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Only matter and energy"},{"id":"b","text":"Only the Triune God"},{"id":"c","text":"God and angels"},{"id":"d","text":"Nothing at all"}],"question":"Who existed before creation?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"creation-1-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"How does God being the Creator affect how you view your own purpose and value?"},"id":"creation-1-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Understand that God is the sole Creator of all things","id":"creation-1","isCompleted":false,"order":1,"title":"God Created Everything","trackId":"doctrine-creation","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Exodus 20:11","text":"For in six days the LORD made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them, and rested on the seventh day.","version":"ESV"},"id":"creation-2-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Genesis 1:5","text":"God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night. And there was evening and there was morning, the first day.","version":"ESV"},"id":"creation-2-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"We believe God created in six literal twenty-four hour periods. The Hebrew word "yom" (day), when used with a number and "evening and morning" (as in Genesis 1), always refers to a normal day. God who is infinite could have created in an instant, but He chose six days to establish a pattern for our work week.

This foundational doctrine matters because it affects our understanding of the authority and clarity of Scripture. If we cannot trust the plain meaning of Genesis, how can we trust the rest of the Bible? The same God who inspired Genesis also inspired the Gospel accounts.","title":"Twenty-Four Hour Days"},"id":"creation-2-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"When the Hebrew word "yom" is used with a number and "evening and morning" (as in Genesis 1), it always refers to a literal 24-hour day.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"The days could mean long ages"},{"id":"b","text":"The Hebrew text uses "day" with numbers and "evening and morning""},{"id":"c","text":"It does not matter how long creation took"},{"id":"d","text":"Science has proven it was millions of years"}],"question":"Why do we believe creation occurred in six literal days?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"creation-2-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"How we interpret Genesis affects our entire approach to Scripture.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"It does not affect anything else"},{"id":"b","text":"If we cannot trust Genesis plainly, we undermine all Scripture"},{"id":"c","text":"It is just a minor detail"},{"id":"d","text":"Science should interpret Scripture"}],"question":"Why does this doctrine matter for trusting Scripture?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"creation-2-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"How does believing in a literal six-day creation affect your trust in the rest of Scripture?"},"id":"creation-2-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Learn that creation occurred in six 24-hour periods","id":"creation-2","isCompleted":false,"order":2,"title":"Six Literal Days","trackId":"doctrine-creation","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"John 1:1-3","text":"In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God. He was in the beginning with God. All things were made through him, and without him was not any thing made that was made.","version":"ESV"},"id":"creation-3-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Genesis 1:2","text":"The earth was without form and void, and darkness was over the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God was hovering over the face of the waters.","version":"ESV"},"id":"creation-3-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Creation was the work of the entire Trinity. The Father planned and commanded (Genesis 1:1). The Son (the Word) executed creation—"all things were made through him" (John 1:3). The Spirit was active in creation, "hovering over the face of the waters" (Genesis 1:2). From the very beginning, we see the Triune God working together in perfect harmony.","title":"The Triune Creator"},"id":"creation-3-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"John 1:3 says all things were made through Jesus (the Word)—nothing was made without Him.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"He had no role—He was created later"},{"id":"b","text":"All things were made through Him"},{"id":"c","text":"He only observed the Father creating"},{"id":"d","text":"He created only living things"}],"question":"According to John 1:3, what role did Jesus play in creation?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"creation-3-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"The Spirit of God was actively present in creation, hovering over the face of the waters.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"He was absent from creation"},{"id":"b","text":"He was hovering over the waters, actively present"},{"id":"c","text":"He only watched"},{"id":"d","text":"He came after creation was complete"}],"question":"What was the Spirit's role in creation according to Genesis 1:2?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"creation-3-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"How does knowing Jesus is your Creator deepen your worship of Him?"},"id":"creation-3-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"See all three Persons of the Trinity active in creation","id":"creation-3","isCompleted":false,"order":3,"title":"The Trinity in Creation","trackId":"doctrine-creation","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Genesis 1:26-27","text":"Then God said, "Let us make man in our image, after our likeness. And let them have dominion..." So God created man in his own image.","version":"ESV"},"id":"creation-4-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Psalm 8:4-5","text":"What is man that you are mindful of him, and the son of man that you care for him? Yet you have made him a little lower than the heavenly beings and crowned him with glory and honor.","version":"ESV"},"id":"creation-4-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Humans are the crown of God's creation, made in His image unlike any other creature. God created man perfect and in His own image by a direct act. Being made in God's image gives every human being dignity, value, and purpose. We were created for relationship with God, to reflect His character, and to exercise loving dominion over the earth.","title":"Made in God's Image"},"id":"creation-4-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Humans were directly created by God in His image—we are not evolved animals but special creations.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Humans are just more evolved animals"},{"id":"b","text":"Humans were directly created in God's image"},{"id":"c","text":"There is nothing unique about humans"},{"id":"d","text":"Humans have bigger brains"}],"question":"What makes humans unique from all other creatures?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"creation-4-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Psalm 8 says God is "mindful" of us and has crowned humanity with "glory and honor."","options":[{"id":"a","text":"With indifference"},{"id":"b","text":"With mindful care, crowning us with glory and honor"},{"id":"c","text":"As insignificant specks"},{"id":"d","text":"As problems to solve"}],"question":"According to Psalm 8, how does God view humanity?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"creation-4-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"How does being made in God's image give your life meaning and purpose?"},"id":"creation-4-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Discover why humans are uniquely special in God's creation","id":"creation-4","isCompleted":false,"order":4,"title":"Humanity: The Crown of Creation","trackId":"doctrine-creation","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Romans 1:20","text":"For his invisible attributes, namely, his eternal power and divine nature, have been clearly perceived, ever since the creation of the world, in the things that have been made.","version":"ESV"},"id":"creation-5-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Psalm 19:1-2","text":"The heavens declare the glory of God, and the sky above proclaims his handiwork. Day to day pours out speech, and night to night reveals knowledge.","version":"ESV"},"id":"creation-5-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Creation is not silent about its Creator! The heavens declare God's glory (Psalm 19:1). The complexity, beauty, and design of nature reveal God's eternal power and divine nature. This is called "general revelation"—enough to leave all people without excuse.","title":"Nature Points to the Creator"},"id":"creation-5-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Romans 1:20 says God's eternal power and divine nature are clearly seen through creation.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Nothing—creation is silent about God"},{"id":"b","text":"His eternal power and divine nature"},{"id":"c","text":"Only scientists can understand it"},{"id":"d","text":"That God does not care"}],"question":"What can people learn about God from creation?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"creation-5-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"a","explanation":"People are without excuse because creation itself clearly reveals God.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Because creation clearly reveals God"},{"id":"b","text":"Because they have heard the gospel"},{"id":"c","text":"Because they own Bibles"},{"id":"d","text":"They actually have an excuse"}],"question":"Why are people "without excuse" according to Romans 1:20?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"creation-5-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"When you look at nature, what specifically reveals God's power or character to you?"},"id":"creation-5-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"See how nature points to the Creator","id":"creation-5","isCompleted":false,"order":5,"title":"Creation Reveals God","trackId":"doctrine-creation","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Genesis 1:28","text":"And God blessed them. And God said to them, "Be fruitful and multiply and fill the earth and subdue it, and have dominion..."","version":"ESV"},"id":"creation-6-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Psalm 24:1","text":"The earth is the LORD's and the fullness thereof, the world and those who dwell therein.","version":"ESV"},"id":"creation-6-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"God gave humanity dominion over creation—not to exploit it, but to steward it wisely. We are caretakers, not owners. The earth belongs to the Lord (Psalm 24:1). We honor God when we care for what He has made.","title":"Stewards of the Earth"},"id":"creation-6-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Dominion means responsible stewardship—caring for God's creation as managers, not owners.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"We can exploit creation however we want"},{"id":"b","text":"We are responsible stewards of God's earth"},{"id":"c","text":"We should worship nature"},{"id":"d","text":"We have no responsibility toward creation"}],"question":"What does human "dominion" over creation mean?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"creation-6-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"c","explanation":"The earth is the LORD's—He owns it all. We are merely stewards.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Humanity"},{"id":"b","text":"No one—it belongs to itself"},{"id":"c","text":"The LORD"},{"id":"d","text":"Governments"}],"question":"According to Psalm 24:1, who owns the earth?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"creation-6-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"How can you be a better steward of God's creation in your daily life?"},"id":"creation-6-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Understand our role as stewards of God's earth","id":"creation-6","isCompleted":false,"order":6,"title":"Our Responsibility to Creation","trackId":"doctrine-creation","xpReward":50}]

name: Satan
description: Know your enemy—his origin, his schemes, and his ultimate defeat
lessons: [{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"John 8:44","text":"You are of your father the devil, and your will is to do your father's desires. He was a murderer from the beginning, and does not stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks out of his own character, for he is a liar and the father of lies.","version":"ESV"},"id":"satan-1-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"1 Peter 5:8","text":"Be sober-minded; be watchful. Your adversary the devil prowls around like a roaring lion, seeking someone to devour.","version":"ESV"},"id":"satan-1-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Satan is not a myth, a symbol of evil, or a cartoon character with horns. He is a real spiritual being—a fallen angel who rebelled against God. We believe in the existence of Satan, the adversary of men and the arch-enemy of God. Jesus spoke of him as a real person—\"a murderer from the beginning\" and \"the father of lies.\"","title":"A Real Enemy"},"id":"satan-1-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"c","explanation":"Satan is a real spiritual being—a fallen angel who rebelled against God.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A fictional character"},{"id":"b","text":"A symbol of human evil"},{"id":"c","text":"A real fallen angel who opposes God"},{"id":"d","text":"An equal to God"}],"question":"According to the Bible, who is Satan?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"satan-1-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Jesus said Satan was \"a murderer from the beginning\" and \"the father of lies.\"","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Love and peace"},{"id":"b","text":"Murder and lying"},{"id":"c","text":"Power and wisdom"},{"id":"d","text":"Beauty and intelligence"}],"question":"According to John 8:44, what two things characterize Satan?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"satan-1-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"How does knowing Satan is real affect how you approach spiritual warfare?"},"id":"satan-1-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Understand that Satan is a real spiritual being","id":"satan-1","isCompleted":false,"order":1,"title":"Satan Is Real","trackId":"doctrine-satan","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Isaiah 14:13-14","text":"You said in your heart, \"I will ascend to heaven; above the stars of God I will set my throne on high... I will make myself like the Most High.\"","version":"ESV"},"id":"satan-2-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Ezekiel 28:15, 17","text":"You were blameless in your ways from the day you were created, till unrighteousness was found in you... Your heart was proud because of your beauty.","version":"ESV"},"id":"satan-2-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Satan was originally a beautiful, powerful angel created by God. But pride filled his heart—five times in Isaiah 14, he says \"I will,\" seeking to exalt himself above God's throne. His sin was the sin of pride and rebellion—wanting to be \"like the Most High.\"","title":"Pride Led to Fall"},"id":"satan-2-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Satan's root sin was pride—he wanted to exalt himself above God.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Jealousy of humans"},{"id":"b","text":"Pride—wanting to be like God"},{"id":"c","text":"He was created evil"},{"id":"d","text":"He made an honest mistake"}],"question":"What was the root sin that caused Satan's fall?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"satan-2-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Satan was originally created blameless and beautiful.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Evil from the start"},{"id":"b","text":"Blameless until unrighteousness was found in him"},{"id":"c","text":"Weak and insignificant"},{"id":"d","text":"Already fallen"}],"question":"How was Satan originally created according to Ezekiel 28:15?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"satan-2-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"Pride was Satan's downfall. How can you guard against pride in your own life?"},"id":"satan-2-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Learn how Satan became God's enemy","id":"satan-2","isCompleted":false,"order":2,"title":"Satan's Fall","trackId":"doctrine-satan","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Genesis 3:1-5","text":"Now the serpent was more crafty than any other beast of the field... He said to the woman, \"Did God actually say...?\"","version":"ESV"},"id":"satan-3-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Matthew 4:3","text":"And the tempter came and said to him, \"If you are the Son of God, command these stones to become loaves of bread.\"","version":"ESV"},"id":"satan-3-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Satan's primary weapon is deception. In Eden, he used a three-step strategy: First, he questioned God's Word (\"Did God actually say?\"). Second, he denied it (\"You will not surely die\"). Third, he twisted it. He still uses these tactics today.","title":"The Deceiver's Tactics"},"id":"satan-3-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Satan's first tactic was causing doubt: \"Did God actually say?\"","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Threatening her with harm"},{"id":"b","text":"Causing doubt about God's Word"},{"id":"c","text":"Offering her money"},{"id":"d","text":"Appearing as a monster"}],"question":"What was Satan's first tactic in tempting Eve?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"satan-3-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Satan used a progression: questioning, denying, then twisting God's Word.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"He threatened her"},{"id":"b","text":"He questioned, denied, and twisted God's Word"},{"id":"c","text":"He forced her hand"},{"id":"d","text":"He appealed to her humility"}],"question":"How did Satan tempt Eve to disobey?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"satan-3-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"In what areas of your life do you sense Satan trying to cause doubt about God's Word?"},"id":"satan-3-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Recognize the devil's tactics against you","id":"satan-3","isCompleted":false,"order":3,"title":"Satan's Schemes","trackId":"doctrine-satan","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Job 1:12","text":"And the LORD said to Satan, \"Behold, all that he has is in your hand. Only against him do not stretch out your hand.\"","version":"ESV"},"id":"satan-4-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"1 John 4:4","text":"Little children, you are from God and have overcome them, for he who is in you is greater than he who is in the world.","version":"ESV"},"id":"satan-4-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Satan is powerful, but he is NOT equal to God! He is a created being with real limitations. He can only do what God permits. In Job, Satan had to get God's permission before acting. And \"greater is He who is in you than he who is in the world\" (1 John 4:4).","title":"A Limited Enemy"},"id":"satan-4-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Satan could only act within the boundaries God set—he is a limited, created being.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Satan is equal to God"},{"id":"b","text":"Satan can only act within limits God permits"},{"id":"c","text":"Satan does not need God's permission"},{"id":"d","text":"Satan is more powerful than God"}],"question":"What do we learn about Satan's power from Job 1:12?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"satan-4-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"The Holy Spirit living in believers is greater than Satan.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"We are stronger than Satan"},{"id":"b","text":"He who is in us (the Holy Spirit) is greater than he who is in the world"},{"id":"c","text":"Satan ignores believers"},{"id":"d","text":"We can negotiate with demons"}],"question":"According to 1 John 4:4, why can believers overcome spiritual forces?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"satan-4-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"How does knowing Satan is limited encourage you when you feel spiritually attacked?"},"id":"satan-4-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Understand that Satan is not equal to God","id":"satan-4","isCompleted":false,"order":4,"title":"Satan's Limitations","trackId":"doctrine-satan","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"James 4:7","text":"Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.","version":"ESV"},"id":"satan-5-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Matthew 4:10-11","text":"Then Jesus said to him, \"Be gone, Satan! For it is written...\" Then the devil left him.","version":"ESV"},"id":"satan-5-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"God has given us everything we need to resist Satan. The key is two-fold: first, submit to God; then resist the devil. Jesus resisted Satan in the wilderness by using Scripture: \"It is written.\" When we stand firm in faith and God's truth, Satan will flee!","title":"Submit and Resist"},"id":"satan-5-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"When we submit to God and resist the devil, he will flee from us!","options":[{"id":"a","text":"He will attack harder"},{"id":"b","text":"He will flee from you"},{"id":"c","text":"Nothing will change"},{"id":"d","text":"He will try to negotiate"}],"question":"According to James 4:7, what is the result of resisting the devil?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"satan-5-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Jesus defeated Satan's temptations by quoting Scripture.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"By debating philosophy"},{"id":"b","text":"By quoting Scripture: \"It is written\""},{"id":"c","text":"By ignoring Satan"},{"id":"d","text":"By performing miracles"}],"question":"How did Jesus resist Satan's temptations in Matthew 4?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"satan-5-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"What practical steps can you take to \"submit to God and resist the devil\" this week?"},"id":"satan-5-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Learn how to stand firm against Satan's attacks","id":"satan-5","isCompleted":false,"order":5,"title":"Resisting the Devil","trackId":"doctrine-satan","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Revelation 20:10","text":"And the devil who had deceived them was thrown into the lake of fire and sulfur... and they will be tormented day and night forever and ever.","version":"ESV"},"id":"satan-6-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Colossians 2:15","text":"He disarmed the rulers and authorities and put them to open shame, by triumphing over them in him.","version":"ESV"},"id":"satan-6-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"The story ends with Satan's total defeat! Jesus already defeated him at the cross—Colossians 2:15 says Christ \"disarmed the rulers and authorities.\" He will be thrown into the lake of fire forever. We are on the winning side—Christ has already won!","title":"The Enemy Is Already Defeated"},"id":"satan-6-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"c","explanation":"Revelation 20:10 says Satan will be thrown into the lake of fire forever.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"He will be forgiven and restored"},{"id":"b","text":"He will rule in hell"},{"id":"c","text":"He will be tormented in the lake of fire forever"},{"id":"d","text":"He will cease to exist"}],"question":"What is Satan's ultimate destiny?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"satan-6-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"a","explanation":"Colossians 2:15 says Christ triumphed at the cross. The victory is already won!","options":[{"id":"a","text":"At the cross"},{"id":"b","text":"At the Second Coming"},{"id":"c","text":"He has not triumphed yet"},{"id":"d","text":"In the Garden of Eden"}],"question":"When did Christ triumph over Satan according to Colossians 2:15?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"satan-6-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"How does knowing Satan's ultimate defeat give you confidence as you face spiritual battles today?"},"id":"satan-6-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Celebrate Christ's victory over the enemy","id":"satan-6","isCompleted":false,"order":6,"title":"Satan's Ultimate Defeat","trackId":"doctrine-satan","xpReward":50}]

name: Humanity & Sin
description: Understand what it means to be human and how sin has affected us all
lessons: [{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Genesis 1:27, 31","text":"So God created man in his own image, in the image of God he created him; male and female he created them... And God saw everything that he had made, and behold, it was very good.","version":"ESV"},"id":"man-1-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Ecclesiastes 7:29","text":"See, this alone I found, that God made man upright, but they have sought out many schemes.","version":"ESV"},"id":"man-1-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"God created man perfect, in His own image, by a direct act. Adam and Eve were created sinless, in perfect fellowship with God and each other. There was no death, no suffering, no shame. This was God's original design for humanity.","title":"Originally Perfect"},"id":"man-1-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"God created humans perfect, in His own image, by a direct act.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Through evolution over millions of years"},{"id":"b","text":"Perfect, in His image, by a direct act"},{"id":"c","text":"With flaws and weaknesses"},{"id":"d","text":"As evolved animals"}],"question":"How did God create the first humans?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"man-1-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"God made humanity upright—the problem is our rebellion.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"We were made flawed"},{"id":"b","text":"God made us upright, but we sought out many schemes"},{"id":"c","text":"Evolution made us imperfect"},{"id":"d","text":"We have no problem"}],"question":"According to Ecclesiastes 7:29, what is humanity's problem?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"man-1-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"How does knowing God's original design was \"very good\" affect how you view human dignity and purpose?"},"id":"man-1-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"See how God originally created humanity","id":"man-1","isCompleted":false,"order":1,"title":"Created Perfect","trackId":"doctrine-man","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Romans 5:12","text":"Therefore, just as sin came into the world through one man, and death through sin, and so death spread to all men because all sinned.","version":"ESV"},"id":"man-2-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Genesis 3:6-7","text":"So when the woman saw that the tree was good for food... she took of its fruit and ate, and she also gave some to her husband who was with her, and he ate.","version":"ESV"},"id":"man-2-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"When Adam sinned, he was acting as the representative of the entire human race. His sin brought physical death (our bodies die) and spiritual death (separation from God) upon all humanity. We all sinned \"in Adam.\"","title":"One Man's Sin Affected All"},"id":"man-2-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Adam's sin brought both physical and spiritual death to the entire human race.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Nothing—each person starts fresh"},{"id":"b","text":"Physical and spiritual death spread to all"},{"id":"c","text":"Only Adam was affected"},{"id":"d","text":"Only physical death resulted"}],"question":"What was the result of Adam's sin for all humanity?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"man-2-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"c","explanation":"Death spread to all men \"because all sinned\"—we all sinned in Adam.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Through evolution"},{"id":"b","text":"Through natural aging"},{"id":"c","text":"Because all sinned in Adam"},{"id":"d","text":"Death is not real"}],"question":"According to Romans 5:12, how did death spread to all men?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"man-2-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"How does understanding the Fall help you make sense of the brokenness you see in the world?"},"id":"man-2-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Understand how sin entered the human race","id":"man-2","isCompleted":false,"order":2,"title":"The Fall","trackId":"doctrine-man","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Romans 3:10-12","text":"As it is written: \"None is righteous, no, not one; no one understands; no one seeks for God. All have turned aside; together they have become worthless; no one does good, not even one.\"","version":"ESV"},"id":"man-3-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Romans 3:23","text":"For all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God.","version":"ESV"},"id":"man-3-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"All men are dead in trespasses and sins, and are unable to remedy their lost condition. Total depravity means every part of us is affected by sin. Our minds, wills, emotions, and bodies are all tainted.","title":"Totally Affected by Sin"},"id":"man-3-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Total depravity means every aspect of our being is affected by sin.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Everyone is as evil as possible"},{"id":"b","text":"Every part of us is affected by sin and we cannot save ourselves"},{"id":"c","text":"Most people are basically good"},{"id":"d","text":"Only bad people are affected by sin"}],"question":"What does \"total depravity\" mean?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"man-3-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"c","explanation":"None is righteous, no, not one.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Most people"},{"id":"b","text":"Some religious people"},{"id":"c","text":"None, not even one"},{"id":"d","text":"Those who try hard"}],"question":"According to Romans 3:10-12, how many people are righteous?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"man-3-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"Why is understanding our inability to save ourselves essential for appreciating the gospel?"},"id":"man-3-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"See how deeply sin has affected every person","id":"man-3","isCompleted":false,"order":3,"title":"Total Depravity","trackId":"doctrine-man","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Psalm 51:5","text":"Behold, I was brought forth in iniquity, and in sin did my mother conceive me.","version":"ESV"},"id":"man-4-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Ephesians 2:3","text":"Among whom we all once lived in the passions of our flesh... and were by nature children of wrath, like the rest of mankind.","version":"ESV"},"id":"man-4-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"David acknowledged that his sinful nature was present from his very conception. We are not born innocent and then corrupted; we are born with a bent toward sin.
