Ephesians 2:3 says we were \"by nature children of wrath.\"","title":"Sinful from the Start"},"id":"man-4-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"c","explanation":"Psalm 51:5 says David was sinful from conception—we are born with a sinful nature.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"When they first commit a sin"},{"id":"b","text":"At puberty"},{"id":"c","text":"From conception—we are born with a sinful nature"},{"id":"d","text":"Only when they choose to sin"}],"question":"According to Psalm 51:5, when does a person become a sinner?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"man-4-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"We were \"by nature children of wrath\"—born into a condition that deserved God's judgment.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Children of light"},{"id":"b","text":"Children of wrath"},{"id":"c","text":"Basically good"},{"id":"d","text":"Neutral"}],"question":"According to Ephesians 2:3, what were we \"by nature\"?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"man-4-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"How does this truth help you understand your own struggles with sin?"},"id":"man-4-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Understand that we are sinners from birth","id":"man-4","isCompleted":false,"order":4,"title":"Born Sinners","trackId":"doctrine-man","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Romans 6:23","text":"For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.","version":"ESV"},"id":"man-5-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Revelation 21:8","text":"But as for the cowardly, the faithless, the detestable... their portion will be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur, which is the second death.","version":"ESV"},"id":"man-5-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Without the remission of sins through the forgiveness of God and the new birth, all men will endure eternal punishment in the lake of fire. The \"wages\" of sin—what sin earns us—is death.","title":"Death Is the Payment"},"id":"man-5-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"The wages of sin is death—every sin deserves eternal separation from God.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Temporary suffering"},{"id":"b","text":"Death—physical, spiritual, and eternal"},{"id":"c","text":"Nothing serious"},{"id":"d","text":"Good works can balance out our sins"}],"question":"What are the \"wages of sin\" according to Romans 6:23?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"man-5-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"c","explanation":"The second death is the lake of fire—eternal conscious punishment.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Physical death"},{"id":"b","text":"Temporary punishment"},{"id":"c","text":"The lake of fire—eternal separation from God"},{"id":"d","text":"Reincarnation"}],"question":"What is the \"second death\" according to Revelation 21:8?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"man-5-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"How does the seriousness of sin make you appreciate the gift of salvation even more?"},"id":"man-5-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"See what our sin truly deserves","id":"man-5","isCompleted":false,"order":5,"title":"The Wages of Sin","trackId":"doctrine-man","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"John 3:3","text":"Jesus answered him, \"Truly, truly, I say to you, unless one is born again he cannot see the kingdom of God.\"","version":"ESV"},"id":"man-6-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"John 3:5-7","text":"Jesus answered, \"Truly, truly, I say to you, unless one is born of water and the Spirit, he cannot enter the kingdom of God... You must be born again.\"","version":"ESV"},"id":"man-6-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Jesus told Nicodemus—a religious, moral man—that he must be born again. Our first birth left us spiritually dead; we need a second birth to receive spiritual life. This is the work of the Holy Spirit.","title":"You Must Be Born Again"},"id":"man-6-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"We must be born again because our first birth left us spiritually dead.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Being religious is enough"},{"id":"b","text":"Our first birth left us spiritually dead; we need new spiritual life"},{"id":"c","text":"It was just a suggestion"},{"id":"d","text":"Good people do not need new birth"}],"question":"Why did Jesus say we \"must be born again\"?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"man-6-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Nicodemus was a Pharisee and teacher—a religious, moral man who still needed new birth.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A notorious sinner who needed saving"},{"id":"b","text":"A religious teacher—showing even \"good\" people need new birth"},{"id":"c","text":"A Roman soldier"},{"id":"d","text":"An atheist"}],"question":"Who was Nicodemus, and why is his encounter with Jesus significant?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"man-6-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"Have you been born again? What evidence of new life do you see in yourself?"},"id":"man-6-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Understand why we must be born again","id":"man-6","isCompleted":false,"order":6,"title":"The Need for New Birth","trackId":"doctrine-man","xpReward":50}]

name: Salvation
description: Discover how God saves lost sinners through faith in Jesus Christ
lessons: [{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Romans 5:8","text":"But God shows his love for us in that while we were still sinners, Christ died for us.","version":"ESV"},"id":"salvation-1-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"John 3:16","text":"For God so loved the world, that he gave his only Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish but have eternal life.","version":"ESV"},"id":"salvation-1-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"We believe that the salvation of lost and sinful men is made possible by the substitutionary sacrifice of Jesus Christ for sinners. We could never save ourselves. But God sent His Son to die for sinners—\"while we were still sinners\"!","title":"Christ Died for Sinners"},"id":"salvation-1-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Salvation is made possible by Christ dying in our place.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Through our good works"},{"id":"b","text":"Through Christ's substitutionary death for sinners"},{"id":"c","text":"Through religious rituals"},{"id":"d","text":"By being born into a Christian family"}],"question":"How is salvation made possible?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"salvation-1-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Christ died for us \"while we were still sinners.\"","options":[{"id":"a","text":"After we became good"},{"id":"b","text":"While we were still sinners"},{"id":"c","text":"When we earned it"},{"id":"d","text":"After we asked for help"}],"question":"According to Romans 5:8, when did Christ die for us?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"salvation-1-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"What does it mean to you that Christ died for you while you were still a sinner?"},"id":"salvation-1-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"See how Christ's sacrifice made salvation available","id":"salvation-1","isCompleted":false,"order":1,"title":"Salvation Made Possible","trackId":"doctrine-salvation","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Ephesians 2:8-9","text":"For by grace you have been saved through faith. And this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God, not a result of works, so that no one may boast.","version":"ESV"},"id":"salvation-2-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Romans 10:9-10","text":"Because, if you confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.","version":"ESV"},"id":"salvation-2-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Salvation is obtained by faith apart from works. It is not earned by being good, attending church, being baptized, or any religious activity. It is a free gift received when we trust in Christ.","title":"By Grace Through Faith"},"id":"salvation-2-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Salvation is by grace through faith, not by works.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Works are necessary to earn salvation"},{"id":"b","text":"Works have no role—salvation is by faith alone"},{"id":"c","text":"Faith plus works equals salvation"},{"id":"d","text":"Only certain works count"}],"question":"What role do works play in obtaining salvation?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"salvation-2-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"a","explanation":"Salvation is not by works \"so that no one may boast\"—God alone deserves glory.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"So that no one may boast"},{"id":"b","text":"Because works do not matter"},{"id":"c","text":"So people can be lazy"},{"id":"d","text":"It does not say this"}],"question":"Why does Ephesians 2:9 say salvation is not by works?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"salvation-2-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"Why is it important that salvation is not based on our works?"},"id":"salvation-2-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Understand that salvation is received by faith, not works","id":"salvation-2","isCompleted":false,"order":2,"title":"Faith Alone","trackId":"doctrine-salvation","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"Acts 20:21","text":"Testifying both to Jews and to Greeks of repentance toward God and of faith in our Lord Jesus Christ.","version":"ESV"},"id":"salvation-3-1","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"reference":"Acts 16:31","text":"And they said, \"Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved, you and your household.\"","version":"ESV"},"id":"salvation-3-1b","type":"scripture"},{"data":{"text":"Salvation is received by a sinner when he repents toward God and places his trust in the person and work of the Lord Jesus. Repentance means a change of mind that leads to a change of direction.","title":"Turning to God"},"id":"salvation-3-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Biblical repentance is a change of mind about sin and self that leads to turning to God.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Just feeling sorry for getting caught"},{"id":"b","text":"A change of mind that turns from sin to God"},{"id":"c","text":"Promising never to sin again"},{"id":"d","text":"Making up for past sins"}],"question":"What is biblical repentance?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"salvation-3-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"\"Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved\"—faith is the requirement.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Be baptized first"},{"id":"b","text":"Believe in the Lord Jesus"},{"id":"c","text":"Do good works"},{"id":"d","text":"Join a church"}],"question":"According to Acts 16:31, what must a person do to be saved?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"salvation-3-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"What did repentance look like in your own experience of coming to Christ?"},"id":"salvation-3-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Learn what it means to truly turn to God","id":"salvation-3","isCompleted":false,"order":3,"title":"Repentance","trackId":"doctrine-salvation","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"John 3:16-18","text":"For God so loved the world, that he gave his only Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish but have eternal life... When we receive Christ, we are born again. This is not a reformation but a transformation. We receive a new nature, new desires, and new life.","title":"New Birth, New Life"},"id":"salvation-5-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Being born again means receiving new spiritual life from the Holy Spirit.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"They try harder to be good"},{"id":"b","text":"They receive new spiritual life from the Holy Spirit"},{"id":"c","text":"They become physically younger"},{"id":"d","text":"Nothing really changes"}],"question":"What happens when a person is \"born again\"?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"salvation-5-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"In Christ, we are \"a new creation\"—the old has passed away; the new has come.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"They are slightly improved"},{"id":"b","text":"They are a new creation—the old has passed away"},{"id":"c","text":"They are the same but forgiven"},{"id":"d","text":"They will change eventually"}],"question":"According to 2 Corinthians 5:17, what has happened to someone \"in Christ\"?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"salvation-5-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"What changes have you noticed in your life since being born again?"},"id":"salvation-5-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Experience the new birth that salvation brings","id":"salvation-5","isCompleted":false,"order":5,"title":"Born Again","trackId":"doctrine-salvation","xpReward":50},{"content":[{"data":{"reference":"John 5:24","text":"Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life. Nothing can separate us from God's love in Christ.","title":"Kept by God's Power"},"id":"salvation-8-2","type":"explanation"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"b","explanation":"Our security rests on God's power keeping us, not our own efforts.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Because we try hard not to sin"},{"id":"b","text":"Because we are kept by God's power, held in the Father's and Son's hands"},{"id":"c","text":"Because we never doubt"},{"id":"d","text":"We are not eternally secure"}],"question":"Why is the believer eternally secure?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"salvation-8-3","type":"question"},{"data":{"correctAnswer":"d","explanation":"NOTHING in all creation can separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus.","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Death or life"},{"id":"b","text":"Angels or rulers"},{"id":"c","text":"Height or depth"},{"id":"d","text":"Nothing in all creation"}],"question":"According to Romans 8:38-39, what can separate us from God's love in Christ?","type":"multiple-choice"},"id":"salvation-8-3b","type":"question"},{"data":{"prompt":"How does the truth of eternal security affect your daily walk with God and your peace of heart?"},"id":"salvation-8-4","type":"reflection"}],"description":"Rest in the assurance that salvation cannot be lost","id":"salvation-8","isCompleted":false,"order":8,"title":"Eternal Security","trackId":"doctrine-salvation","xpReward":50}]
